method: ADR 0009 D5 — record the Linux CI baseline FIRST; baseline-derived ceilings are baseline p95 plus fixed headroom, set once per roadmap cycle, never ratcheted per item. A preregistered absolute target remains the ceiling when comparable evidence lands below it. Scenarios without maxP95Ms are in RECORDING mode: the bench reports them and cannot fail on them. A targetP95Ms records that target but does not arm enforcement until comparable evidence exists.

runner.measurement: hook cold fallback uses a fresh full process; hook resident warm uses a fresh lightweight launcher per sample against the opt-in resident MCP evaluator after one discarded prime and never caches a verdict; cold and one-shot-warm doctor use identical fresh-process argv/tree/cache state; resident doctor starts a fresh ark-check client per sample, reuses only canonical facts, and recomputes live doctor surfaces; --no-cache remains an explicit legacy flag; non-zero child exits abort the run

hook.rationale: Compatibility/recovery path: ark-mcp --hook validates the single proposed file in a fresh full process. Phase Y's 683.761 ms baseline remains the comparison point. Phase Z source 6fa5079 CI run 29649631288 measured 591.165 ms and 595.053 ms on unchanged-head attempts; the frozen 900 ms ceiling retains 51.25% headroom over the worse Phase Z observation and is never ratcheted per item (ADR 0009 D5).

hookResidentWarm.rationale: Z07 source 224a39a / PR synthetic candidate a3d7306, CI run 29700873361 established the 41.487 ms resident baseline. Final source d5cbe61 / synthetic candidate 381e51b, CI run 29704311754 measured 58.177 ms p95 at 10k after one discarded prime. The fresh launcher used the opt-in resident MCP evaluator, cached no verdict, preserved exact output bytes, and left the fixture tree unchanged. The frozen 65 ms ceiling retains 11.73% runner headroom and the final result is 11.75x below the retained Phase Y 683.761 ms baseline.

doctorCold.rationale: Cold one-shot doctor over the tree. Phase Y's 5,154.522 ms baseline remains the retained comparison point. Phase Z source 6fa5079 CI run 29649631288 measured 5,072.093 ms and 4,799.834 ms on unchanged-head attempts; the frozen 6,800 ms ceiling retains 34.06% headroom over the worse Phase Z observation and is never ratcheted per item (ADR 0009 D5).

doctorOneShotWarm.rationale: Z07 source 778a33a / PR synthetic candidate 9cfcb3a, CI run 29699589991, established the 2,174.517 ms p95 like-for-like baseline from fresh doctor processes after one discarded prime. Final source d5cbe61 / CI run 29704311754 recorded 2,616.594 ms with the exact cold argv and immutable fixture/cache state. This remains a cache-free one-shot control, not a resident or 10x claim.

doctorResidentWarm.rationale: Z07 source d5cbe61 / PR synthetic candidate 381e51b, CI run 29704311754 measured 468.669 ms p95 on Linux x64 Node 20 at 10k after one discarded prime. A lightweight public launcher removed one-shot module loading from the resident client while preserving exact cold JSON and fallback behavior. The canonical-facts pilot cached no verdict, reused only the immutable snapshot, and retained exact edit/delete/invalidation parity. The frozen 500 ms absolute UX ceiling retains 6.69% headroom; the like-for-like 2,174.517 ms one-shot baseline makes this a 4.64x improvement, not a 10x doctor claim.
